title: Glistening Syrian Cheese Flatbread
description: A savory flatbread with melted akkawi cheese, smoky lard, and fragrant herbs, perfect for a quick lunch.
instructions: In a bowl combine flour, salt, and yeast.
Add warm water and mix until a soft dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth.
Cover the dough, let it rise for 30 minutes until doubled in size.
Preheat a cast iron skillet over medium heat.
Heat the lard in the skillet until it melts and begins to smoke slightly.
Divide the dough into four equal portions and roll each into a thin circle.
Place a portion of dough onto the hot skillet and cook for 2 minutes on each side until golden.
Top the cooked flatbread with slices of akkawi cheese, tomato puree, and a sprinkle of oregano.
Cover the skillet with a lid and cook for an additional 2 minutes until the cheese melts.
Drizzle the flatbread with the remaining melted lard and sprinkle with fresh parsley.
Serve hot with a side of flatbread chips.
